page.title: polycystic kidney disease — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for polycystic kidney disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for polycystic kidney disease: 166 alterations and 143 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive; GFR decline averages 4-5 mL/min/year in Mayo Class 1C-1E; renal failure by 60-70 years in 50% without treatment; spontaneous stabilisation rare
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Tolvaptan (Jynarque): slows GFR decline by 35% and total kidney volume growth by 49% (TEMPO 3:4, REPRISE); dietary protein restriction + hydration: modest benefit; SGLT2 inhibitors — early signal in PKD trials
remission.gap_size: Moderate — tolvaptan is available and effective but hepatotoxicity risk limits use; access barriers (REMS programme in US) reduce prescribing
remission.barrier_detail: Tolvaptan REMS programme (liver toxicity monitoring) creates prescribing complexity; only patients with rapidly progressive disease (Mayo Class 1C-1E) are eligible by guideline; most patients remain on supportive care without disease-modifying therapy
remission.last_soc_change: 2018 — tolvaptan FDA approved for ADPKD; TEMPO 3:4 published 2012; mTOR inhibitors (sirolimus, everolimus) tried and failed in RCTs 2010; supportive care unchanged for decades until tolvaptan
remission.outdated_treatments: mTOR inhibitors (sirolimus/everolimus for ADPKD — Phase 3 failed, abandoned); routine caffeine restriction beyond adequate hydration (no evidence); aggressive aggressive blood pressure treatment to very low targets without individualised risk assessment
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Dietary: plain water intake >3L/day (reduces AVP stimulation, slows cyst growth — based on tolvaptan mechanism); sodium restriction; avoidance of nephrotoxins; SGLT2 inhibitors (early signal in ADPKD from Phase 2 trials — STAGED-PKD underway)
remission.frontier_signal: Lixivaptan (V2R antagonist without hepatotoxicity, Phase 3 ALERT trial underway); bardoxolone methyl (NRF2 activation — Phase 3 CARDINAL Alport positive; ADPKD Phase 2 ongoing); mTOR inhibitors with tissue-specific targeting; targeted gene silencing (anti-PC2 siRNA in murine models)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
